departments.description:
Operations — Description

Operations is responsible for executing FLI Golf events with precision, consistency, and safety. The operations function coordinates people, processes, and physical assets to ensure each stadium-style event runs smoothly from setup through teardown while delivering a professional experience for players, fans, partners, and media.
departments.name: Operations

departments.description:
Marketing & Public Relations (PR) — Description

Marketing & PR drives awareness, demand, and brand credibility for the FLI Golf League by shaping how the league is presented to fans, media, sponsors, and partners. The strategy blends storytelling, live-event promotion, digital marketing, and media relations to position FLI Golf as a modern, must-watch sports entertainment brand.
departments.name: Marketing & PR

departments.description:
Sales — Description

The Sales function drives all revenue generation for the FLI Golf League by converting fan interest, brand partnerships, and live events into diversified, scalable income streams. Sales strategy is built around direct-to-consumer revenue, high-value partnerships, and recurring subscriptions, supported by data-driven pricing and bundled offerings.
departments.name: Sales

departments.description:
Finance — Description

The Finance function oversees all financial planning, controls, and reporting for the FLI Golf League, ensuring fiscal discipline while supporting rapid growth. Finance is responsible for budgeting, revenue management, cost control, and financial transparency across league operations.
departments.name: Finance

departments.description:
Production: Film Live Our Event — Description

“Film Live Our Event” is a fully integrated live-production operation designed to capture, produce, and distribute the FLI Golf stadium event experience in real time. The production model transforms the competition into a broadcast-ready sports property, delivering cinematic coverage, live storytelling, and sponsor-ready media assets.
departments.name: Production

departments.description:
Course Construction — Description

Course Construction covers the physical build-out of the FLI Golf stadium-style disc golf course, transforming an open venue into a safe, visually compelling, and competition-ready arena. The construction process prioritizes modularity, durability, speed of installation, and broadcast visibility while maintaining elite-level playing standards.
departments.name: Course Construction

departments.description:
Technology (Tech) — Description

The Technology (Tech) layer powers the entire FLI Golf ecosystem, enabling real-time scoring, live media distribution, fan engagement, commerce, and league operations. The tech stack is designed to be modular, scalable, and reliable, supporting live stadium events while extending the league’s reach through digital platforms.
departments.name: Technical

projects.description:
FLI Golf Clothing Launch — Description

The FLI Golf Clothing Launch introduces an official lifestyle and performance apparel line designed to extend the FLI Golf brand beyond the course and into everyday wear. The launch blends sport, fashion, and fan identity, positioning the clothing line as both a revenue driver and a long-term brand pillar.
projects.name: Launch All Clothing

projects.description:
Stadium-Style Disc Golf Course — Build-Out Description

A stadium-style disc golf course is a purpose-built competition environment designed to transform disc golf into a live, ticketed spectator experience. Unlike traditional park-based courses, this format emphasizes visibility, fan engagement, broadcast quality, and premium hospitality while maintaining elite-level competitive integrity.
projects.name: Build Disc Golf Course

projects.description:
Product & Feature Overview

FLI Golf is a fully integrated digital platform powering the business, fan engagement, and monetization of a stadium-style disc golf league. The platform combines live events, fantasy competition, merchandise, and premium experiences into a single ecosystem designed to scale with the league.
projects.name: App & Website Refactor

projects.description:
Production: Film Live Our Event — Description

“Film Live Our Event” is a fully integrated live-production operation designed to capture, produce, and distribute the FLI Golf stadium event experience in real time. The production model transforms the competition into a broadcast-ready sports property, delivering cinematic coverage, live storytelling, and sponsor-ready media assets.
projects.name: Production Equipment Quote

projects.description:
Legal Framework — League Launch Overview

Launching the FLI Golf League requires a structured legal foundation that protects the league, its participants, partners, and investors while enabling scalable growth. The legal framework establishes governance, compliance, risk management, and commercial rights across all league operations.
projects.name: Legal Framework

projects.description:
Sponsor Acquisition Strategy

FLI Golf approaches sponsor acquisition as a structured, data-driven partnership program, not one-off sponsorship sales. Sponsors are acquired through a repeatable pipeline managed within FliHub, ensuring consistent outreach, qualification, activation, and renewal.
projects.name: Acquire sponsors

projects.description:
Marketing Details

FLI Golf’s marketing strategy is built around live entertainment, digital storytelling, and data-driven fan engagement. Rather than relying solely on traditional sports marketing, the league operates as a content-first brand, using events, media, and technology to acquire, engage, and retain fans, sponsors, and partners.

All marketing initiatives are planned, executed, and measured through FliHub, ensuring consistency across channels and real-time performance tracking.
projects.name: Marketing Details

projects.description:
Revenue Streams

FLI Golf is designed as a multi-channel revenue organization, combining live events, digital products, media distribution, and commerce into a diversified and scalable revenue model. All revenue streams are managed, tracked, and optimized through FliHub, ensuring transparency, automation, and real-time performance insights.

Ticket Sales & Live Event Access

FLI Golf generates direct consumer revenue through tiered ticketing for stadium-style disc golf events.

Ticket offerings include:

  • General admission access

  • Reserved seating and premium viewing areas

  • Multi-day event passes

  • Group and corporate ticket packages

Digital ticketing, access control, and attendance tracking are managed centrally, allowing demand-based pricing and post-event analytics.
projects.name: Revenue streams

projects.description:
Operations Details

FLI Golf operates as a technology-enabled sports and entertainment organization, with centralized operational control managed through FliHub, our proprietary internal management platform. All league operations—on-site execution, logistics, staffing, media, and financial workflows—are coordinated through structured, role-based systems to ensure consistency, accountability, and scalability.
projects.name: Operation details

tasks.subTasksChecklist:
​
### Checklist for Forming Relationships with News Outlets
#### 1. Research and Identification
* [ ] **Research Analyst:​**
#### 2. Initial Outreach
* [ ] **Outreach Coordinator:​**
#### 3. Building Relationships
* [ ] **Public Relations Manager:​**
#### 4. Content Preparation
* [ ] **Content Writer:​**
- [ ] **Graphic Designer:​**
#### 5. Pitching Stories
* [ ] **Media Relations Specialist:​**
#### 6. Follow-up and Engagement
* [ ] **Follow-up Coordinator:​**
#### 7. Providing Value
* [ ] **Industry Expert/Spokesperson:​**
#### 8. Monitoring and Tracking
* [ ] **Media Monitoring Specialist:​**
#### 9. Crisis Management
* [ ] **Crisis Communications Manager:​**
#### 10. Continuous Relationship Building
* [ ] **Relationship Manager:​**
#### Example Checklist for a Single News Outlet
**Outlet: Disc Golf Monthly**
1. **Research and Identification:​**
   * Identify key journalists: John Doe (Sports Editor), Jane Smith (Feature Writer).
   * Compile contact details and LinkedIn profiles.
2. **Initial Outreach:​**
   * Draft and send introductory email to John Doe and Jane Smith.
   * Follow up with a phone call to introduce the brand and schedule a meeting.
3. **Building Relationships:​**
   * Schedule a virtual coffee chat with John Doe.
   * Engage with Jane Smith’s articles on social media.
4. **Content Preparation:​**
   * Create a press release about the app’s new feature.
   * Design an infographic showcasing user statistics.
5. **Pitching Stories:​**
   * Send a personalized pitch to John Doe highlighting the app’s impact on player performance.
   * Offer an exclusive interview with the app’s founder to Jane Smith.
6. **Follow-up and Engagement:​**
   * Send a follow-up email one week after the initial pitch.
   * Comment on John Doe’s recent article about disc golf trends.
7. **Providing Value:​**
   * Offer John Doe an exclusive first look at the app’s upcoming update.
   * Provide Jane Smith with expert insights on the latest disc golf statistics.
8. **Monitoring and Tracking:​**
   * Track mentions of the app in Disc Golf Monthly.
   * Update the media interaction database with outcomes from pitches.
9. **Crisis Management:​**
   * Prepare a statement addressing any potential issues with the app.
   * Ensure John Doe and Jane Smith receive the statement promptly.
10. **Continuous Relationship Building:​**
    * Send a thank-you note to John Doe and Jane Smith after coverage.
    * Invite them to the app’s next product launch event.
tasks.title: Begin Public Relation Strategy

### Checklist for Finalizing and Closing Sponsors
1. **Finalize Current Sponsors:​**
   * Compile and confirm the current sponsor list.
   * Ensure all agreements are signed.
   * Confirm deliverables and recognition plans.
   * Maintain regular communication and seek feedback.
2. **Close New Sponsors:​**
   * Identify and research potential sponsors.
   * Develop tailored sponsorship packages.
   * Craft and send personalized pitches.
   * Schedule and conduct meetings.
   * Negotiate terms and close deals.
   * Onboard new sponsors and integrate them into event planning.

​
**Inter**:
* **Style**: Sans-serif
* **Why**: Inter is a modern, highly legible font with a clean and minimalistic look. It's optimized for web and mobile interfaces, making it a great choice for projects focused on clarity and readability.
​
**Lora**:
* **Style**: Serif
* **Why**: Lora is a well-balanced serif font that offers both a contemporary feel and a touch of elegance. It works beautifully for text-heavy content, giving your project a sophisticated and classic appearance.
**Poppins**:
* **Style**: Geometric Sans-serif
* **Why**: Poppins is a geometric sans-serif font with a clean and modern aesthetic. Its strong and rounded forms make it versatile for both headings and body text, adding a dynamic and friendly vibe to your design.

tasks.subTasksChecklist:
​
### Local Business Outreach
#### 1. Identify Target Businesses
* **Research Local Businesses:​**
  * Create a list of local businesses that align with your goals and audience.
  * Focus on businesses that would benefit from partnering with or sponsoring your event or campaign.
* **Categorize Businesses:​**
  * Group businesses by industry (e.g., retail, restaurants, fitness centers) for tailored outreach strategies.
#### 2. Develop Outreach Materials
* **Create a Pitch Deck:​**
  * Highlight the benefits of partnering with your organization.
  * Include details about your event or campaign, audience demographics, and potential ROI for the business.
* **Prepare Marketing Collateral:​**
  * Design brochures, flyers, and business cards to leave with potential partners.
  * Ensure all materials are branded and convey your message clearly.
#### 3. Initial Contact
* **Email Outreach:​**
  * Draft personalized emails to introduce your organization and propose a meeting.
  * Highlight potential benefits and suggest a few dates for a follow-up call or in-person meeting.
* **Phone Calls:​**
  * Follow up on emails with phone calls to schedule meetings.
  * Be prepared to discuss your proposal and answer any questions.
#### 4. Meetings and Presentations
* **Schedule Meetings:​**
  * Arrange in-person or virtual meetings with key decision-makers.
  * Prepare a tailored presentation for each business.
* **Present Your Proposal:​**
  * Highlight how the partnership can benefit the business.
  * Provide examples of successful past collaborations if available.
#### 5. Formalize Partnerships
* **Draft Agreements:​**
  * Prepare partnership agreements outlining the terms and benefits for both parties.
  * Include details on deliverables, timelines, and expectations.
* **Secure Commitments:​**
  * Obtain signed agreements and confirm the business’s participation.
  * Provide a timeline for deliverables and ongoing communication.
### College Outreach
#### 1. Identify Target Colleges
* **Research Local Colleges:​**
  * Create a list of local colleges and universities.
  * Identify relevant departments, clubs, and organizations that align with your goals.
* **Connect with Key Contacts:​**
  * Identify key contacts such as department heads, club leaders, and career services staff.
#### 2. Develop Outreach Materials
* **Create Informational Packets:​**
  * Develop packets that include information about your organization, event, or campaign.
  * Highlight opportunities for student involvement, internships, or sponsorship.
* **Prepare Presentations:​**
  * Design presentations tailored to different audiences within the college (e.g., students, faculty, administration).
#### 3. Initial Contact
* **Email Outreach:​**
  * Draft personalized emails to key contacts introducing your organization and proposing a meeting.
  * Suggest potential collaboration ideas and benefits for the college.
* **Phone Calls:​**
  * Follow up on emails with phone calls to schedule meetings.
  * Be prepared to discuss your proposal and answer any questions.
#### 4. Campus Visits and Presentations
* **Schedule Campus Visits:​**
  * Arrange visits to campuses to meet with key contacts and present your proposal.
  * Attend relevant campus events and fairs to network and promote your organization.
* **Present Your Proposal:​**
  * Highlight how the partnership can benefit the college and its students.
  * Provide examples of successful past collaborations if available.
#### 5. Formalize Partnerships
* **Draft Agreements:​**
  * Prepare partnership agreements outlining the terms and benefits for both parties.
  * Include details on deliverables, timelines, and expectations.
* **Secure Commitments:​**
  * Obtain signed agreements and confirm the college’s participation.
  * Provide a timeline for deliverables and ongoing communication.
### Example Timeline for Outreach Program
**Month 1:​**
* Research and identify target businesses and colleges.
* Develop outreach materials (pitch deck, brochures, informational packets).
**Month 2:​**
* Initiate email and phone outreach to schedule meetings.
* Prepare tailored presentations for each meeting.
**Month 3:​**
* Conduct meetings and presentations.
* Draft and finalize partnership agreements.
**Month 4:​**
* Secure commitments and formalize partnerships.
* Begin planning and executing collaborative activities.
### Checklist for Local Business and College Outreach
**Local Business Outreach:​**
* Identify target businesses.
* Develop a pitch deck and marketing collateral.
* Draft and send personalized emails.
* Follow up with phone calls.
* Schedule and conduct meetings.
* Present your proposal.
* Draft partnership agreements.
* Secure commitments.
**College Outreach:​**
* Identify target colleges and key contacts.
* Develop informational packets and presentations.
* Draft and send personalized emails.
* Follow up with phone calls.
* Schedule campus visits and attend events.
* Present your proposal.
* Draft partnership agreements.
* Secure commitments.
tasks.title: Begin local business and college outreach
